notice_set_password: Set a password in settings before enabling protection.
setting_folder_desc: The default value '/' is the root path of the vault
setting_toggle_desc: Turn on to lock your vault with a password. Turn off to remove protection (your password is required).
hint_enter_in_both_boxes: Enter your password in both fields.
enter_password_to_verify: Enter your password to unlock the vault.
auto_lock_interval_desc: Minutes of inactivity before the vault auto-locks. Set to 0 to disable.
before_open_protection: Settings below are locked while protection is enabled.
setting_pwd_hint_question_desc: A question shown alongside the unlock prompt to help you remember your password.
setting_more_path: More folders or files to be protected (up to 6)
notice_locked_write_blocked: This file is encrypted and the vault is locked. Unlock first to edit it.
notice_decrypt_failed: Failed to decrypt {{path}}. The file may be corrupted or encrypted with a different password.
setting_mode_desc: Session: files stay plaintext on disk and only the Obsidian UI is locked. Encrypted: files are written as ciphertext on disk; if you forget your password the data cannot be recovered.
bulk_encrypt_confirm_body: Every Markdown file under {{folder}} will be rewritten as ciphertext. If you forget your password these files become unreadable.
bulk_decrypt_confirm_body: Every encrypted Markdown file under {{folder}} will be rewritten as plaintext.
bulk_reencrypt_confirm_body: Every encrypted file will be rewritten with the new password. Do not close Obsidian during this operation.
bulk_failures_console_hint: See the developer console for per-file error details.
mode_flip_to_encrypted_title: Switch this folder to encrypted mode?
mode_flip_to_encrypted_body: New files in {{folder}} will be encrypted on disk. Encrypt the {{count}} existing plaintext file(s) now? You can also do this later from settings.
mode_flip_to_session_title: Switch this folder to session mode?
mode_flip_to_session_body: New files in {{folder}} will be plaintext on disk. The {{count}} already-encrypted file(s) will stay encrypted unless you decrypt them now.
disable_guard_body: Disabling protection while files are encrypted would make them unreadable. Decrypt all encrypted files first, then disable.
setting_change_password_desc: Re-encrypts all encrypted files with the new password. Cannot be undone.
change_password_no_encrypted_files: No encrypted files found. Updating password only.
picker_truncated: Showing {{shown}} of {{total}} matches — refine your search to see more.
protected_paths_section_desc: Pick folders or notes to protect, set a mode for each, and run bulk encrypt/decrypt as needed.
protected_paths_empty: No paths added yet. Click "Choose paths…" to get started.
protected_paths_remove_tooltip: Remove this path. Already-encrypted files stay encrypted on disk.
notice_unlock_first: Unlock the vault before encrypting or decrypting files.
notice_mode_set_protect_off: Mode set. Turn on password protection at the top of this page to actually encrypt the files.
notice_mode_set_locked: Mode set. Click the lock icon in the ribbon to unlock the vault, then come back to encrypt the files.
notice_mode_set_no_key: Mode set, but the encryption key is not loaded. Try reloading the plugin.
help_toggle_body:
Turn this on to lock your vault behind a password. Anyone — including you — needs the password to open protected notes.

To turn it off later, you'll have to type your current password to confirm.

If you have any encrypted notes, you'll be asked to decrypt them first before turning protection off — otherwise you'd lose access to them.

⚠️ **If you forget your password, encrypted notes can't be recovered.** Pick something you'll remember.
help_autolock_body:
Re-locks your vault automatically after you stop using it for a while.

Type the number of **minutes** of idle time before locking. For example, `5` means "lock after 5 minutes without opening any protected note."

Set to `0` to never auto-lock — your vault stays open until you click the lock icon in the sidebar or close Obsidian.
help_pwhint_body:
A short question shown when you type the wrong password. It helps you remember without telling someone else.

✅ Good: `first dog's name`
✅ Good: `the album from college`
❌ Bad: writing the password itself here

This hint is saved as plain text in the plugin's settings file, so don't write anything secret in it.
help_changepw_body:
Updates your password. If you have encrypted notes, they're re-locked with the new password too.

1. Type your current password.
2. Type the new password twice.
3. The plugin re-locks your encrypted notes — keep Obsidian open until it finishes.

If something interrupts the operation (Obsidian closes, your computer sleeps), just run *Change password* again with the **same new password** — it picks up where it left off.
help_paths_body:
## Picking what to protect

Click **Choose paths…** to pick folders or notes from a list. Picking a folder protects everything inside it.

## Two protection modes

**Session lock** is like locking the screen.

✅ Your notes stay normal on disk
✅ Search and graph still work
✅ Safe — you can't lose notes if you forget the password

**Encryption** actually scrambles the file's content.

✅ Real privacy across devices and other apps
⚠️ Search won't find words inside encrypted notes
⚠️ **If you forget your password, encrypted notes are gone forever**

## A few more things

- The mode you set on a folder applies to **new** notes inside it. To convert existing notes, use the *Encrypt all* / *Decrypt all* button on that row.
- Moving an encrypted note out of an encrypted folder does **not** unscramble it. Right-click the file → *Decrypt this file* to convert it back.
- If folders are nested with different modes, the more specific (closer) folder wins.
